label: Felrapportering — Sentry
summary: Production error tracking + performance monitoring via Sentry. Adds three init shims (client / server / edge) plus an instrumentation entry that wires them into Next.js. Self-disables when NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN is empty so dev/preview environments do not ship beacons. Use when the brief asks for crash reporting, observability, 'know when the site breaks', or production-grade monitoring.
summarySv: Felrapportering via Sentry som meddelar dig när något går sönder på den publicerade sajten, utan att besökarna behöver höra av sig. Utan nyckel stänger blocket av sig självt helt (ingen demoyta), vilket är det förväntade beteendet.

envVars.purpose: Sentry Data Source Name (e.g. 'https://abc@o12345.ingest.sentry.io/67890'). Sentry → Settings → Projects → Client Keys (DSN). Tagged 'warn-only' because Sentry.init() short-circuits when empty — SDK bundled but inert, no crash.

Logical environment tag attached to events (e.g. 'production', 'preview', 'staging'). Defaults to NODE_ENV when unset. Set explicitly in Vercel preview deploys so preview crashes do not pollute the production project.

Float 0.0-1.0 controlling what fraction of transactions are sent for performance tracing. Defaults to 0.1 (10%). Lower for high-traffic sites to keep the Sentry quota in check; raise to 1.0 only on low-volume internal apps.

notes: All four files mount at the project root, NOT under app/ or components/. Inject paths: sentry.client.config.ts, sentry.server.config.ts, sentry.edge.config.ts, instrumentation.ts — the file names are load-bearing for Next.js' instrumentation hook to find them. Documenting via instructions.md because the schema does not have a 'rename-on-emit' field.
